Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

1. We will lose opportunity to move towards new k8s especially that the k8s 15 is no longer supported. Moving towards new k8s is one of main requirements for Guilin.
2. There is no prove that the AAI E2E test cases toped to work due to new readiness image
All health checks done after two commits for readiness are PASSED
[AAI] Use readiness container v3.0.1 https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/aai/oom/+/112227
Sep 07 10:37 PM - Patch Set 2 results: https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//112227-2/index.html
[GENERAL] Use readiness container v3.0.1 https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/111509
Sep 04 5:36 PM - Patch Set 13 results: https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//111509-13/index.html
No trace of any healthcheck error neither on AAI nor SO after using new readiness.
3. According to Morgan’s statement in https://jira.onap.org/browse/SO-3216 "So we may say that 111381 broke master at least from the End to End test perspective" 
We may assume that AAI E2E tests stopped to work after this commit: https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/aai/oom/+/111381
Unfortunately the test results from this run are not available.

I propose to revert AAI changes from this commit https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/aai/oom/+/111381

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> 1. We will lose opportunity to move towards new k8s especially that the k8s 15 is no longer supported. Moving towards new k8s is one of main requirements for Guilin.
> 2. There is no prove that the AAI E2E test cases toped to work due to new readiness image
> All health checks done after two commits for readiness are PASSED
> [AAI] Use readiness container v3.0.1 https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/aai/oom/+/112227
> Sep 07 10:37 PM - Patch Set 2 results: https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//112227-2/index.html
> [GENERAL] Use readiness container v3.0.1 https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/111509
> Sep 04 5:36 PM - Patch Set 13 results: https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//111509-13/index.html
> No trace of any healthcheck error neither on AAI nor SO after using new readiness.
> 3. According to Morgan’s statement in https://jira.onap.org/browse/SO-3216 "So we may say that 111381 broke master at least from the End to End test perspective" 
> We may assume that AAI E2E tests stopped to work after this commit: https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/aai/oom/+/111381
> Unfortunately the test results from this run are not available.
> 
> I propose to revert AAI changes from this commit https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/aai/oom/+/111381

@Grzegorz
No worries this is purely for testing purpose. We'll not make such a huge revert. We just want to find a commit which broke the e2e testing so we build this test machine to make a test gating run

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> 1. We will lose opportunity to move towards new k8s especially that the k8s 15 is no longer supported. Moving towards new k8s is one of main requirements for Guilin.
> 2. There is no prove that the AAI E2E test cases toped to work due to new readiness image
> All health checks done after two commits for readiness are PASSED
> [AAI] Use readiness container v3.0.1 https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/aai/oom/+/112227
> Sep 07 10:37 PM - Patch Set 2 results: https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//112227-2/index.html
> [GENERAL] Use readiness container v3.0.1 https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/111509
> Sep 04 5:36 PM - Patch Set 13 results: https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//111509-13/index.html
> No trace of any healthcheck error neither on AAI nor SO after using new readiness.
> 3. According to Morgan’s statement in https://jira.onap.org/browse/SO-3216 "So we may say that 111381 broke master at least from the End to End test perspective" 
> We may assume that AAI E2E tests stopped to work after this commit: https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/aai/oom/+/111381
> Unfortunately the test results from this run are not available.

BTW.
We already tested this and unfortunately it's not only AAI that broke e2e testing. We already tested reverting related AAI commits and e2e test were still broken.

The issue was hidden for quite some time due to race condition in tests so now we need to figure out a point in the time where it got broken first.

> 
> I propose to revert AAI changes from this commit https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/aai/oom/+/111381
